description: Figure 1 displays the initial microstructure of the experimental low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel (1.46 wt.% C, 25.14 wt.% Mn, 10 wt.% Al, 0.053 wt.% Nb, balance Fe), obtained using a LEXT OLS4000 laser confocal microscope after standard grinding, polishing, and etching with 30% nitric acid alcohol solution (volume fraction). The micrograph exhibits a polycrystalline grain structure with distinct grain boundaries and prominent annealing twins within the austenitic matrix, indicative of the as-homogenized and forged condition. Grain sizes are non-uniform across the field of view, ranging from relatively fine to coarse grains, with a scale bar of 100 μm for reference. This as-received microstructure establishes the critical baseline for subsequent isothermal hot compression studies performed on a Gleeble-3500 simulator at temperatures of 900–1150 °C and strain rates of 0.01–10 s⁻¹, enabling quantitative comparison of dynamic recrystallization kinetics, grain refinement, and microstructural evolution induced by thermomechanical processing.
